initialStates.description: Exactly one initial state (filled circle). Flow always starts here.

id.description: Unique id; must be used as source in exactly one transition to the first real state.

finalStates.description: One or more final states (bullseye). Whenever a state can end, add a transition to one of these.

id.description: Unique id; use as target in transitions when flow terminates.

states.description: Normal states (rounded rectangles). Each has an id and label.

id.description: Unique id; referenced in transitions and optionally in compositeStates.childIds.

compositeStates.description: Optional. At least one composite state when the flow has a logical grouping (e.g. Processing containing Validating and Executing). childIds list state ids that appear inside this composite.

id.description: Unique id for the composite (not used as transition source/target; only for grouping).

childIds.description: Ids of states that are nested inside this composite. Each must appear in states.

transitions.description: Directed transitions. Each state (and initial/final) must have at most 2 incoming and at most 2 outgoing transitions to keep the diagram clean. source: initial or state id; target: state or final id. Prefer the most important flows.

source.description: Id of source node (initial state or state).

target.description: Id of target node (state or final state).

label.description: Event/trigger name shown on the arrow (e.g. start, submit, timeout, done).

eventParameter.description: Optional. Shown as event (eventParameter), e.g. submit (formData), timeout (30s).
